research.question: What was the actual numeric fidelity failure mode in turn 675—did the encoder strip "42.3" to "423" via decimal removal, or did it first remove the decimal point then concatenate digits? The grader shows 42.3→423, 5.1→51, 3.8→38, 78.0→780—consistent pattern: remove decimal, keep all digits contiguous. This suggests the encoder treats decimal points as omittable punctuation under rule-036, not as value-preserving syntax.
